title: Process priorities — low/normal/high/max scheduling
purpose: BEAM processes have four priority levels: low, normal, high, and max. Higher-priority processes are scheduled before lower-priority ones. The scheduler dequeues from max first, then high, then normal, then low. This is used by OTP for time-critical processes (e.g., net_kernel, code_server). Without priorities, all processes compete equally — which is fine for most workflows but blocks OTP modules that set process priorities.
task: Add a priority field to Process, implement priority-aware run queue dequeuing, and register the `erlang:process_flag(priority, Level)` BIF extension.

requirements.title: Priority field and enum
requirements.spec: Add a `Priority` enum and a `priority` field to Process.
acceptance: Priority is settable at spawn and via process_flag
Test: new process has Normal priority

requirements.title: Priority-aware run queue dequeuing
requirements.spec: Modify the run queue to dequeue higher-priority processes first.
acceptance: Run queue maintains separate internal queues per priority level (or uses priority-based ordering)
Dequeue order: Max → High → Normal → Low
Low-priority processes run at least once every N scheduling rounds (anti-starvation, N configurable, default 8)
Test: enqueue max and normal processes, verify max dequeues first
Test: low-priority process makes progress alongside high-priority processes

requirements.title: process_flag(priority, Level) BIF extension
requirements.spec: Extend the existing `erlang:process_flag/2` BIF to handle the `priority` flag.
acceptance: `process_flag(priority, max)` sets the process priority to Max, returns previous priority
Test: set priority to high, verify process_info(priority) returns high

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement priority inheritance or priority inversion prevention
SHALL NOT starve low-priority processes indefinitely — guarantee progress within a bounded window
SHALL NOT change the work-stealing algorithm — priorities are per-queue, not per-steal
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
